title: SDL citations sidecar
description: Cross-reference annotations sidecar for an SDL page. Lives alongside the matching *.sdl.yaml (same directory, `<page>.citations.yaml`). Holds the human-curated metadata (per-transition / per-subroutine `notes` + `references`, plus page-level `pinned_refs`) that the tool-driven `Packet.Sdl.Transcribe` doesn't emit but is preserved so it survives regeneration. Keys under `transitions:` / `subroutines:` are transition ids or subroutine names from the matching `*.sdl.yaml`. When tool-regenerated yaml changes transition ids (e.g. branch-label suffix change), the corresponding citation key needs re-matching — currently a manual / LLM-assisted pass per the convention agreed 2026-05-18.

pinned_refs.description: Per-source git ref pins for cross-reference citations. Each entry binds a source identifier (e.g. 'linbpq') to the repo URL and the specific commit at which line numbers in `references[].line` are valid. Pinning to commits stops citations going stale when the upstream repos refactor.

commit.description: Commit hash, tag, or other immutable ref at which the citation's line numbers are valid.

transitions.description: Citation annotations keyed by transition id (matching the `id:` field of a transition in the corresponding `<page>.sdl.yaml`). Only state-page sidecar files populate this.

subroutines.description: Citation annotations keyed by subroutine name (matching the `name:` field of a subroutine in the corresponding subroutines `<page>.sdl.yaml`). Only the figc4.7-style subroutines sidecar populates this.

notes.description: Free-form notes about the transition or subroutine — typically the spec-prose reading and any non-obvious figure quirks. Multi-paragraph allowed.

references.description: Cross-reference citations. `spec_prose` entries cite AX.25 spec text; other source entries cite implementation code in pinned repos (commit at page-level `pinned_refs[source]`). `function` is the primary anchor — survives refactors that shift line numbers.

source.description: Origin of the citation. 'spec_prose' for the AX.25 specification text; any other value must have a matching entry in the file's `pinned_refs`.

cite.description: spec_prose only: section + paragraph reference, e.g. '§6.3.5 ¶1'.

quote.description: spec_prose only: verbatim text from the cited paragraph.

path.description: Code citations: path of the cited file within the source repo.

function.description: Code citations: function/method name containing the cited logic. Primary anchor — survives refactors that shift line numbers.

note.description: Optional short comment explaining what to look at in the cited code.
